audienceProblem: Which tasks should I keep as a chat, handle with AI assistance, or delegate to an agent?
centralThesis: Upgrade a task from chat to agent delegation when it has a concrete output, accessible context and tools, trustworthy feedback, and bounded risk; task length alone is not enough.

reusableArtifact.name: Task Upgrade Scorecard
gates: Contiguous action chain with accessible context and tools
Observable evidence and a verifiable definition of done
Bounded permissions, reversibility, and explicit approval points

P0: The unit of work shifts from an answer to a delegated workflow
Task fit depends on output, context, feedback, and risk rather than duration alone
The reusable Task Upgrade Scorecard and its four work modes
P1: How to package context and define environmental feedback
Where human review belongs for irreversible or high-impact actions
How repeated work becomes a reusable skill or workflow
P2: Directional adoption and task-horizon evidence from Codex usage
Examples across research, documents, spreadsheets, publishing, and software work
Parallel work only when tasks are independent and review remains tractable
P3: Token-share tables that do not change the task decision
Occupational background not needed to use the scorecard

chapters.promise: Replace the vague question of what agents can do with a task decision
See why agent work is measured in delegated workflows, not chats
Use four task properties to judge whether delegation can work
Choose chat, copilot, supervised agent, or delegated workflow
Give the agent enough ground truth to make progress
Place human judgment where mistakes become expensive
Turn repeated delegation into a dependable operating system
Remember the four gates and upgrade only when they hold

roles.engineer: Defines the outcome, packages context, sets permissions, reviews evidence, and approves irreversible actions
roles.tinyAgent: Inspects context, uses tools, executes steps, reads environmental feedback, and returns a verifiable artifact

sharedVisualSemantics: Long task horizon as evidence, not a delegation rule
Four task gates: output, context, feedback, and risk
Four work modes on the Task Upgrade Scorecard
Risk ladder with approval before irreversible actions
Repeated procedures becoming reusable skills and parallel workflows
languagePolicy: English and Chinese share thesis, priorities, chapter order, fact boundaries, character tasks, and the reusable artifact. Wording and timing are authored independently.
